zh.lifeSummary: 當Ortún Ortíz de Zúñiga Fernandez於1203年出生在西班牙納法洛亞尼加時，他的父親Lope Ortiz de Zúñiga y Azagra Señor de Mendavia30歲，母親Tercia Fernández de Santiago22歲。他於1224年與Teresa Rada De Beaumont在西班牙納法洛亞尼加奧尼加結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1270年享年67歲，埋葬在西班牙納法洛亞。

zh-hans.Amusco, Palencia, Castilla y León, Españ: 西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞阿姆斯可

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ortún Ortíz de Zúñiga Fernandez于1203年出生在西班牙納法洛亞尼加时，他的父亲Lope Ortiz de Zúñiga y Azagra Señor de Mendavia30岁，母亲Tercia Fernández de Santiago22岁。他于1224年与Teresa Rada De Beaumont在西班牙納法洛亞尼加奧尼加结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1270年享年67岁，埋葬在西班牙納法洛亞。

nameMeanings.name: Ortíz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Angel, Jorge, Pedro, Ramon.
Spanish: patronymic from the Basque personal name Orti, from Latin Fortunius (see Fortuno ). Compare Urtiz .

History: Nicolas Ortiz Ladron de Guevara, a native of Mexico City, came to Santa Fe, NM, in June 1694 with his wife and children and other families recruited to recolonize New Mexico after the Pueblo Indian revolt.

variants.name: Fortuno
meanings: Spanish (Fortuño): from the personal name Fortuño, from Latin Fortunius, derived from fortuna ‘luck’.

variants.name: Orquiz
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): perhaps an altered form of Spanish   Ortiz  .

variants.name: Ortis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Agustin, Ana, Arturo, Baldomero, Bicente, Consuelo, Emiliano, Ernestina, Gerardo, Jesus, Luis Enrique.
Spanish: variant of   Ortiz  .

variants.name: Ortez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Ana, Carlos, Margarita, Mario, Sergio, Adela, Adriana, Amando, Aminta, Andres.
Spanish: variant of Ortiz or of Catalan Ortes, plural form of Orta .

variants.name: Urtiz
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Spanish   Ortiz  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Zúñiga
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Jorge, Miguel, Pedro, Alberto, Francisco, Javier.
Basque (Zuñiga, Castilianized as Zúñiga): habitational name from a place in Navarre named Zuñiga (in Castilian: Zúñiga), from Basque zuin ‘cultivated field’ + iga ‘incline, slope’. Compare Suniga .

variants.name: Suniga
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Jose, Raul, Ruben, Xavier, Abelardo, Agapito, Alberto, Ana, Anselma, Benito, Domingo.
Hispanic (also Súñiga): altered form of Basque Zúñiga (see Zuniga ). This surname is also found in the Philippines (compare Sunga ).

variants.name: Sunga
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ramon, Jose, Luis, Orlando, Roberto, Amado, Augusto, Eduardo, Eloisa, Elvira, Erlinda, Ernesto.
Hispanic (Philippines; also Suñga): altered form of Basque Zúñiga (see Zuniga ). Compare Suniga .

nameMeanings.name: Fernandez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Jorge, Miguel, Fernando, Mario, Jesus, Pedro, Francisco.  Portuguese Ligia, Joao, Armanda, Godofredo, Wenceslao.

Spanish (Fernández): patronymic from the personal name Fernando . The surname (and to a lesser extent the variant Hernandez ) has also been established in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some Jewish descendants retained the surname that their families had adopted as Roman Catholics.

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Orlando, Aida, Carlos, Jesusa, Rodolfo, Ruben, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.  Indian Rohan, Lalith, Nimal, Rajiv, Sanjeeva, Shanthi, Anil, Anusha, Dushan, Kapila, Lakshman, Mallika.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Fernando, a younger form of Ferdinando (see Ferdinand ). This surname is also found in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there, and as a result of the expulsion of the Jews from Spain and Portugal at the end of the 15th century, many of whom moved to Italy. It is also common in western India and especially in Sri Lanka, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Ortún
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Orton in Cambridgeshire, Leicestershire, Northamptonshire, Warwickshire, and Westmorland. All those in England share a second element from Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’, but the first element in each case is more difficult to determine. Examples in Cambridgeshire and Warwickshire are on the banks of rivers, so these are probably derived from Old English ōfer ‘riverbank’; in other cases it is impossible to distinguish between ofer ‘ridge’ and ufera ‘upper’. Orton in Westmorland is probably formed with the Old Norse byname Orri ‘black-cock’ (the male black grouse). Orton near Fochabers, Scotland, is of uncertain etymology.

Americanized form of Norwegian Årtun: habitational name from the farm name Årtun, found in six places, e.g. in the province of Rogaland, a compound of the genitive case singular of Old Norse á ‘small river’ and tún ‘farm yard (surrounded by buildings)’.

variants.name: Urton
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from any of the numerous places in England so called. In most cases the place name is derived from Old English burh ‘fortress, fortified place’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

en.lifeSummary: When Ortún Ortíz de Zúñiga Fernandez was born in 1203, in Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Spain, his father, Lope Ortiz de Zúñiga y Azagra Señor de Mendavia, was 30 and his mother, Tercia Fernández de Santiago, was 22. He married Teresa Rada De Beaumont in 1224, in Zúñiga, Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Spain. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1270, at the age of 67, and was buried in Nafarroa, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ortún Ortíz de Zúñiga Fernandez est né en 1203, à Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Espagne, son père, Lope Ortiz de Zúñiga y Azagra Señor de Mendavia, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Tercia Fernández de Santiago, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Teresa Rada De Beaumont en 1224, à Zúñiga, Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1270, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Nafarroa, Espagne.

de.Amusco, Palencia, Castilla y León, Españ: Amusco, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spanien
de.lifeSummary: Als Ortún Ortíz de Zúñiga Fernandez 1203 in Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lope Ortiz de Zúñiga y Azagra Señor de Mendavia 30 und seine Mutter Tercia Fernández de Santiago 22. Er hat 1224 in Zúñiga, Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Spanien Teresa Rada De Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1270 im Alter von 67 Jahren und wurde in Nafarroa, Spanien begraben.

Lui sposato Teresa Rada De Beaumont nel 1224, nel Zúñiga, Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1270, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Nafarroa, Spagna.

ko.lifeSummary: Ortún Ortíz de Zúñiga Fernandez(이)가 1203년 스페인 나파로 주n익아에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lope Ortiz de Zúñiga y Azagra Señor de Mendavia(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Tercia Fernández de Santiago(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1224년 스페인 나파로 주n익아 즈un익아에서 Teresa Rada De Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1270년 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 나파로에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Teresa Rada De Beaumont em 1224, em Zúñiga, Zuñiga, Nafarroa, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.Amusco, Palencia, Castilla y León, Españ: амуско, пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ortún Ortíz de Zúñiga Fernandez родился в 1203 г. в местности зуnига, нафарроа, Испания, его отцу по имени Lope Ortiz de Zúñiga y Azagra Señor de Mendavia было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Tercia Fernández de Santiago, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Teresa Rada De Beaumont в 1224 г. в местности зunига, зуnига, нафарроа, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1270 г. в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности нафарроа, Испания.

Se casó con Teresa Rada De Beaumont en 1224, en Zúñiga, Zúñiga, Navarra, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1270, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Navarra, España.
